core_model.structure_summary: Critical points of an energy functional — a nonlocal fractional-order quadratic form plus a coefficient-weighted quadratic term minus a spatially modulated nonlinear potential term — are sought on the manifold of functions with fixed squared integral norm, the constraint's multiplier appearing as an unknown eigenvalue-like parameter in the associated nonlocal elliptic equation.
core_model.canonical_form: (-\Delta)^s u + V(\varepsilon x)\, u = \lambda u + h(\varepsilon x) f(u) \ \text{in } \mathbb{R}^N, \qquad \int_{\mathbb{R}^N} |u|^2\, dx = a; \qquad I(u) = \tfrac{1}{2}\int |(-\Delta)^{s/2} u|^2 + \tfrac{1}{2}\int V(\varepsilon x) u^2 - \int h(\varepsilon x) F(u)

variables.meaning: scalar function on unbounded continuum space, the unknown critical point
unknown multiplier enforcing the fixed-norm constraint, determined together with the solution
prescribed value of the squared integral norm
small scaling parameter dilating the two bounded coefficient functions; solution count tied to the number of global maxima of the coefficient modulating the nonlinear term
energy functional whose constrained critical points are the sought solutions

notes: The 'diffusion_term' tag refers to a fractional (nonlocal) power of the Laplacian, s in (0,1), not a literal second derivative. The model can equivalently be typed as a stationary nonlocal elliptic PDE with an unknown multiplier; the main multiplicity results are proved variationally on the fixed-norm sphere with subcritical-growth nonlinearity.
